key_insight: LLM reasoning can be modeled as settling into deep attractor basins, where correct chains are stable minima and hallucinations are shallow, unstable ones.
engineering_relevance: A Gibbs-weighted sampling-and-reweighting inference scheme can improve mathematical reasoning reliability without changing the base model.
method: Sample multiple reasoning trajectories from the model.
Assign each path an energy and weight it by $P\propto e^{-\beta E}$.
Aggregate weighted paths to approximate the equilibrium solution of the associative memory.
evidence_signal: On GSM8K, Microsoft Phi-3.5 improved from 84.7% to 90.1%, a 5.38% gain according to the abstract.
watch_item: The approach may add inference cost and its gains could depend on how energy, spectral entropy, and the temperature parameter $\beta$ are defined and tuned.
paper_title: Reasoning as Attractor Dynamics: Latent Memory Retrieval via Gibbs-Weighted Energy Minimization
